title: cymbidium eburneum var. dayanum
intro: Cymbidium eburneum var. dayanum, also known as the Day's cymbidium or Day's orchid, is a type of flowering orchid native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia, characterized by its showy white flowers and strong, sweet fragrance. Traditionally, Cymbidium eburneum var. dayanum has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions such as wounds and burns. In traditional herbal preparations, the flowers and leaves of the Cymbidium eburneum var. dayanum are used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which are often used to treat a range of ailments.
